The file `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py` around the remote divergence check (likely around lines 260-280) 2. The imports at the top of that file 3. Any existing submit pipeline test files
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.

session.assistant: Good! I found the file. Now let me look for relevant documentation about PR operations and the submit pipeline. Let me check those categories:
Great! I found relevant documentation on submit phases and validation. Let me read those docs first:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: PR Submit Workflow Phases 3→read_when: 4→ - "understanding the erk pr submit workflow" 5→ - "debugging PR submission issues" 6→ - "working with AI-generated PR descriptions" 7→ - "understanding plan context integration in PRs" 8→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 9→audit_result: clean 10→--- 11→ 12→# PR Submit Workflow Phases 13→ 14→The `erk pr submit` command uses a 6-phase workflow to submit PRs with AI-generated descriptions. This document explains each phase and the two-layer architecture. 15→ 16→## Two-Layer Architecture 17→ 18→The workflow has two layers: 19→ 20→1. **Core layer (always runs)**: `git push` + `gh pr create` - works without Graphite 21→2. **Graphite layer (optional)**: `gt submit` for stack metadata - runs when Graphite is available and branch is tracked 22→ 23→## Workflow Phases 24→ 25→| Phase | Name | Description | 26→| ----- | ------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | 27→| 1 | Creating or Updating PR | Push changes and create/find PR | 28→| 2 | Getting diff | Extract diff from GitHub API for AI analysis | 29→| 3 | Fetching plan context | Get plan from linked erk-plan issue | 30→| 4 | Generating PR description | AI generates title and body via Claude CLI |
... [121 lines omitted] ...
104→This phase is non-fatal - errors are warnings, not failures.

1→--- 2→title: PR Validation Rules 3→read_when: 4→ - "debugging 'erk pr check' failures" 5→ - "building or modifying PR submission pipelines" 6→ - "generating PR bodies with checkout footers" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "using issue number from .erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json in a checkout footer" 9→ warning: "Checkout footers require the PR number (from create_pr return value), NOT the plan issue number. See pr-validation-rules.md." 10→last_audited: "2026-02-26 00:00 PT" 11→audit_result: edited 12→--- 13→ 14→# PR Validation Rules 15→ 16→`erk pr check` enforces structural invariants on every PR: a checkout footer containing the correct PR number. Understanding **why** these checks exist and **where** the data comes from prevents the most common agent mistakes. 17→ 18→## The Checks 19→ 20→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/check_cmd.py, pr_check --> 21→ 22→See `pr_check()` in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/check_cmd.py` for the orchestration logic. The command runs checks in sequence: 23→ 24→| Check | What it validates | Why it exists | 25→| ----------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | 26→| Plan-ref presence | `.erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json` exists and contains a valid plan ID | Confirms the PR is linked to a tracked plan | 27→| Checkout footer | PR body contains `erk pr checkout <pr_number>` with exact number | Lets reviewers check out the PR into a fresh worktree | 28→| Header position | Plan-header metadata is at bottom, not legacy top position | Ensures PR body follows current formatting standards | 29→ 30→## The PR Number vs Issue Number Trap
... [56 lines omitted] ...
32→This is the single most common validation failure for agents, and it stems from a data source confusion: 49→## Debugging Validation Failures 57→Common failure modes: 70→| `.erk/impl-context/` cleanup | Directory must not exist in the repo | Transient artifacts cause CI formatter failures (Prettier) |

1→--- 2→title: Graphite Divergence Detection 3→read_when: 4→ - "debugging remote divergence errors during erk pr submit" 5→ - "understanding the Graphite-first submit flow's pre-checks" 6→ - "resolving 'branch is behind remote' errors" 7→ - "using --force flag with erk pr submit" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "running gt commands without --no-interactive" 10→ warning: "All gt commands MUST use --no-interactive. Without it, gt may prompt for input and hang indefinitely." 11→ - action: "pushing to a branch that may have been updated remotely without checking for divergence" 12→ warning: "The Graphite-first flow pre-checks for divergence before gt submit. Check with branch_exists_on_remote -> fetch_branch -> is_branch_diverged_from_remote." 13→ - action: "running raw git commands (checkout, branch) without gt track on a Graphite-managed branch" 14→ warning: "Raw git commands (checkout, branch) without `gt track` cause Graphite's cache to diverge from actual branch state. Use BranchManager which handles Graphite tracking automatically, or call `gt track` after raw git operations." 15→ score: 7 16→--- 17→ 18→# Graphite Divergence Detection 19→ 20→The Graphite-first submit flow includes proactive divergence detection before running `gt submit`. This prevents cryptic failures when the remote branch has been updated by CI, another session, or a prior submission. 21→ 22→## Guard Chain 23→ 24→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py:200-230 --> 25→ 26→The `_graphite_first_flow()` function in `submit_pipeline.py` runs a four-step guard chain before `gt submit`: 27→ 28→``` 29→branch_exists_on_remote() → fetch_branch() → is_branch_diverged_from_remote() → check behind > 0 30→```
... [48 lines omitted] ...
35→4. **Check `behind > 0`**: If the local branch is behind remote AND `effective_force` is `False`, return a `SubmitError` with `error_type="remote_diverged"`. Note: `effective_force = state.force or is_plan_impl`, so plan implementations (`state.issue_number is not None`) auto-force and never hit this guard — it only applies to non-plan `erk pr submit` invocations. 43→| On divergence | Rebase automatically | Error with resolution steps | 49→When divergence is detected, the error message suggests two options: 66→When `state.force` is `True` (from `erk pr submit -f`), the divergence check is bypassed entirely. The guard chain still runs `fetch_branch()` but skips the error return when `behind > 0`. This allows force-pushing over remote changes when the user explicitly opts in. 72→- [Draft PR Handling — Troubleshooting](../pr-operations/draft-pr-handling.md#troubleshooting-common-failures) - Related failure modes

200→ push happens later in push_and_create_pr. 201→ """ 202→ if not impl_context_exists(state.repo_root): 203→ return state 204→ 205→ if not ctx.git.status.has_tracked_files(state.repo_root, ".erk/impl-context"): 206→ return state 207→ 208→ remove_impl_context(state.repo_root) 209→ ctx.git.commit.stage_files(state.repo_root, [".erk/impl-context/"], force=True) 210→ ctx.git.commit.commit(state.repo_root, "Remove .erk/impl-context/ before submission") 211→ 212→ return state 213→ 214→ 215→def commit_wip(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError: 216→ """Check for uncommitted changes; if present, add_all + commit.""" 217→ if ctx.git.status.has_uncommitted_changes(state.cwd): 218→ if not state.quiet: 219→ click.echo(click.style(" Committing uncommitted changes...", dim=True)) 220→ ctx.git.commit.add_all(state.cwd) 221→ ctx.git.commit.commit(state.cwd, "WIP: Prepare for PR submission") 222→ return state 223→ 224→ 225→def capture_existing_pr_body(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError: 226→ """Capture full PR body before gt submit overwrites it.""" 227→ if not state.quiet: 228→ click.echo(click.style(" Checking for existing PR...", dim=True)) 229→ pr_result = ctx.github.get_pr_for_branch(state.repo_root, state.branch_name)

252→def _graphite_first_flow(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError: 253→ """Graphite-first flow: gt submit handles push + PR creation.""" 254→ if not state.quiet: 255→ click.echo(click.style("Phase 1: Graphite Submit", bold=True)) 256→ 257→ # Auto-force for plan implementations (branches always diverge from remote). 258→ # Fall back to branch prefix when cleanup already deleted .erk/impl-context/ 259→ # but the first submit attempt failed before pushing. 260→ is_plan_impl = state.plan_id is not None or state.branch_name.startswith("plnd/") 261→ effective_force = state.force or is_plan_impl 262→ 263→ # Pre-check: detect remote divergence before gt submit 264→ if ctx.git.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(state.repo_root, "origin", state.branch_name): 265→ ctx.git.remote.fetch_branch(state.repo_root, "origin", state.branch_name) 266→ divergence = ctx.git.branch.is_branch_diverged_from_remote( 267→ state.cwd, state.branch_name, "origin" 268→ ) 269→ if divergence.behind > 0 and not effective_force: 270→ ahead_msg = ( 271→ f" and ahead by {divergence.ahead} commit(s)" if divergence.ahead > 0 else "" 272→ ) 273→ return SubmitError( 274→ phase="push_and_create_pr", 275→ error_type="remote_diverged", 276→ message=( 277→ f"Branch '{state.branch_name}' is behind remote by " 278→ f"{divergence.behind} commit(s){ahead_msg}.

89→def test_submit_failure_returns_error(tmp_path: Path) -> None: 90→ """SubmitError(error_type='graphite_submit_failed') on RuntimeError.""" 92→ submit_stack_raises=RuntimeError("gt submit failed"), 109→ assert isinstance(result, SubmitError) 110→ assert result.error_type == "graphite_submit_failed" 113→def test_restack_error_returns_actionable_message(tmp_path: Path) -> None: 114→ """SubmitError(error_type='graphite_restack_required') when restack needed.""" 116→ submit_stack_raises=RuntimeError( 117→ "gt submit failed (exit code 1): " 118→ "ERROR: You must restack and resolve conflicts with gt restack before submitting." 136→ assert isinstance(result, SubmitError) 137→ assert result.error_type == "graphite_restack_required" 142→def test_pr_not_found_after_submit_returns_error(tmp_path: Path) -> None: 143→ """SubmitError(error_type='pr_not_found') when no PR after gt submit.""" 162→ assert isinstance(result, SubmitError) 163→ assert result.error_type == "pr_not_found" 202→ """Plan impl branch (plan_id set) auto-forces when behind remote; no error returned.""" 308→def test_non_plan_branch_errors_on_divergence(tmp_path: Path) -> None: 309→ """Non-plan branch (no plan_id) still errors when behind remote.""" 331→ assert isinstance(result, SubmitError) 332→ assert result.error_type == "remote_diverged" 335→def test_branch_behind_remote_returns_error(tmp_path: Path) -> None: 336→ """SubmitError(error_type='remote_diverged') when branch is behind remote.""" 360→ assert isinstance(result, SubmitError) 361→ assert result.error_type == "remote_diverged"

session.assistant:
Perfect! I now have all the information you need. Let me create a comprehensive summary.
## Summary
I've found the submit pipeline file and documented the remote divergence pre-check section. Here's what you requested:
### 1. **File Location and Remote Divergence Check**
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py` (1058 lines total)
**Remote Divergence Pre-Check Section (lines 263-289)** in `_graphite_first_flow()`:
```python def _graphite_first_flow(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError: """Graphite-first flow: gt submit handles push + PR creation.""" if not state.quiet: click.echo(click.style("Phase 1: Graphite Submit", bold=True))
# Auto-force for plan implementations (branches always diverge from remote). # Fall back to branch prefix when cleanup already deleted .erk/impl-context/ # but the first submit attempt failed before pushing. is_plan_impl = state.plan_id is not None or state.branch_name.startswith("plnd/") effective_force = state.force or is_plan_impl
# Pre-check: detect remote divergence before gt submit if ctx.git.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(state.repo_root, "origin", state.branch_name): ctx.git.remote.fetch_branch(state.repo_root, "origin", state.branch_name) divergence = ctx.git.branch.is_branch_diverged_from_remote( state.cwd, state.branch_name, "origin" ) if divergence.behind > 0 and not effective_force: ahead_msg = ( f" and ahead by {divergence.ahead} commit(s)" if divergence.ahead > 0 else "" ) return SubmitError( phase="push_and_create_pr", error_type="remote_diverged", message=( f"Branch '{state.branch_name}' is behind remote by " f"{divergence.behind} commit(s){ahead_msg}.\n\n" "The remote branch has been updated (e.g., by CI or another session).\n\n" "To fix:\n" " erk pr reconcile-with-remote --dangerous # Reconcile with remote\n" " erk pr submit -f # Force push" ), details={ "branch": state.branch_name, "ahead": str(divergence.ahead), "behind": str(divergence.behind), }, ) ```
### 2. **Imports at Top of File (lines 1-61)**
```python """Linear pipeline for PR submission.
Replaces the dual-path branching orchestration with a sequence of plain functions transforming a frozen SubmitState dataclass.
Each step: (ErkContext, SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError """
import dataclasses import sys import uuid from collections.abc import Callable from dataclasses import dataclass from functools import cache from pathlib import Path
import click
from erk.cli.commands.pr.shared import ( assemble_pr_body, echo_plan_context_status, maybe_advance_lifecycle_to_impl, recover_plan_header, run_commit_message_generation, ) from erk.cli.constants import PLANNED_PR_TITLE_PREFIX from erk.cli.ensure import UserFacingCliError from erk.core.commit_message_generator import CommitMessageGenerator from erk.core.context import ErkContext from erk.core.plan_context_provider import PlanContext, PlanContextProvider from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PullRebaseError, PushError from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueNotFound from erk_shared.gateway.github.label_ops import add_labels_resilient from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( extract_metadata_value, extract_raw_metadata_blocks, has_metadata_block, replace_metadata_block_in_body, ) from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.roadmap import ( render_objective_roadmap_block, rerender_comment_roadmap, update_node_in_frontmatter, ) from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.types import BlockKeys from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import parse_git_remote_url from erk_shared.gateway.github.pr_footer import build_pr_body_footer from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import BodyText, GitHubRepoId, PRNotFound from erk_shared.gateway.gt.operations.finalize import ERK_SKIP_LEARN_LABEL, is_learn_plan from erk_shared.gateway.gt.prompts import truncate_diff from erk_shared.gateway.pr.diff_extraction import filter_diff_excluded_files from erk_shared.gateway.pr.graphite_enhance import should_enhance_with_graphite from erk_shared.impl_context import impl_context_exists, remove_impl_context from erk_shared.impl_folder import ( has_plan_ref, read_plan_ref, resolve_impl_dir, save_plan_ref, validate_plan_linkage, ) from erk_shared.scratch.scratch import write_scratch_file ```
### 3. **Data Types (lines 87-123)**
The pipeline uses two frozen dataclasses:
```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class SubmitState: """Immutable state threaded through the submit pipeline.""" cwd: Path repo_root: Path branch_name: str parent_branch: str trunk_branch: str use_graphite: bool force: bool debug: bool session_id: str skip_description: bool quiet: bool plan_id: str | None pr_number: int | None pr_url: str | None was_created: bool base_branch: str | None graphite_url: str | None diff_file: Path | None plan_context: PlanContext | None title: str | None body: str | None existing_pr_body: str
@dataclass(frozen=True) class SubmitError: """Error result from a pipeline step.""" phase: str error_type: str message: str details: dict[str, str] ```
### 4. **Submit Pipeline Test Files**
Complete test file specifically for the graphite-first flow:
- **Primary:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_graphite_first_flow.py` (363 lines) - Contains 10 unit tests covering: - Success path - Graphite submit failures - Restack error handling - PR not found after submit - Plan impl auto-forcing on divergence - `plnd/` branch prefix auto-forcing - Branch not on remote (skip divergence check) - Non-plan branch errors on divergence - Branch behind remote error
- **Other related test files:** - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_core_submit_flow.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_cleanup_impl_for_submit.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit_graphite_disabled.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit_pr_cache_polling.py`
### 5. **Related Documentation**
I also found excellent documentation explaining the architecture:
- **`docs/learned/erk/graphite-divergence-detection.md`** — Explains the guard chain and divergence detection logic with resolution steps - **`docs/learned/pr-operations/pr-submit-phases.md`** — Documents the 6-phase workflow and internal 10-step pipeline architecture - **`docs/learned/pr-operations/pr-validation-rules.md`** — Explains PR validation and the PR number vs issue number distinction
All these files are in the read-only code repository.
